method_atom: An analysis of the spectral function associated with the fluctuations of a conserved-current/stress quantity, extracting a low-frequency transport coefficient from the zero-frequency limit of that spectral function. Analytic progress is made in two limiting regimes — weak coupling and the neighborhood of a second-order critical point — where a narrow peak emerges at low frequency. It also addresses the ill-posed inverse problem of analytically continuing an imaginary-time (Euclidean) correlation function, sampled with statistical error, to recover the real-frequency spectral function.
object_atom: The trace of the stress-energy tensor in QCD (the strongly-interacting quark-gluon medium) and its bulk viscosity, with the Euclidean correlator obtained from lattice-QCD data.
brief_reason: The spectral-function/transport-coefficient extraction and the Euclidean-to-real-frequency analytic-continuation machinery carry across fields, while the QCD stress tensor, its bulk viscosity, and lattice data are the domain-specific object.
